subcategories.label: Dog Bite
subcategories.law: Control of Dogs Act 1986 — strict liability applies. The owner is liable regardless of whether the dog had previously bitten anyone.
subcategories.time_limit: 2 years from the date of the incident
subcategories.compensation_range: €10,000 to €40,000 for moderate injuries. Serious cases involving scarring or psychological trauma can be significantly higher.

stories.name: Aoife
stories.situation: Bitten by a neighbour's dog that had slipped its lead in a communal green. The owner insisted the dog had never done anything like this before.

stories.name: Tomás
stories.situation: A gas meter reader bitten on a routine visit. His employer initially told him it was his own fault for not reading the warning signs properly.

stories.name: Blessing
stories.situation: Knocked to the ground and bitten by a large dog that broke free from its owner during a walk. Sustained bruising and a deep bite on her calf.

stories.name: Piotr
stories.situation: Delivery driver bitten at a rural property. The dog had been in the garden unattended. The owner had no home insurance but the claim still proceeded.

subcategories.label: Car or Road Traffic Accident
subcategories.law: Road Traffic Act — drivers owe a duty of care to other road users. Claims go through the Motor Insurers Bureau of Ireland (MIBI) if the driver is uninsured.
subcategories.time_limit: 2 years from the date of the accident
subcategories.compensation_range: €15,000 to €100,000+ depending on injury severity, loss of earnings, and long-term impact.

stories.name: Ciara
stories.situation: Rear-ended at a red light by a driver who admitted he was looking at his phone. Ciara suffered whiplash and missed three weeks of work.

stories.name: Seamus
stories.situation: Hit by a car while crossing at a pedestrian crossing. The driver claimed the lights were amber. CCTV footage told a different story.

stories.name: Natalia
stories.situation: Passenger in a car driven by a friend who lost control on a wet road. She was reluctant to claim against her friend but her solicitor explained how insurance works.

stories.name: Kevin
stories.situation: Hit from the side at a junction by a driver who jumped a stop sign. Kevin's car was written off and he had a fractured wrist.
stories.outcome: Compensation included vehicle damage and injury

subcategories.label: Slipped or Fell in a Shop or Public Place
subcategories.law: Occupiers Liability Act 1995 — businesses and property owners owe a duty of care to visitors. Failure to maintain safe premises creates liability.
subcategories.time_limit: 2 years from the date of the incident
subcategories.compensation_range: €10,000 to €60,000 depending on injury, loss of earnings, and medical costs.

stories.name: Siobhan
stories.situation: Slipped on a wet floor in Lidl. No warning sign, no cone. Staff were apologetic but the company denied liability initially.
stories.outcome: Settled — fractured wrist, medical costs covered

stories.name: Marek
stories.situation: Tripped on a broken floor tile in a shopping centre. He reported it to security but was told the tile had only just been noticed that day.
stories.outcome: Compensation awarded including loss of earnings

stories.name: Patricia
stories.situation: Fell on an unmarked step at the entrance of a restaurant. The lighting was poor and there was no high-visibility strip on the edge of the step.

stories.name: Ahmed
stories.situation: Slipped on a wet surface outside a supermarket during a rain shower. No mat, no warning, no non-slip surface at the entrance.

subcategories.label: Injured at Work or on a Job Site
subcategories.law: Safety, Health and Welfare at Work Act 2005 — employers have a statutory duty to provide a safe workplace, safe equipment, and adequate training.
subcategories.time_limit: 2 years from the date of the injury
subcategories.compensation_range: €20,000 to €150,000+ depending on severity, long-term impact on ability to work, and employer negligence.

stories.name: Brendan
stories.situation: Fell from scaffolding on a construction site. No safety harness had been provided. His employer claimed he should have requested one.

stories.name: Olga
stories.situation: Warehouse worker who injured her back lifting heavy boxes without any manual handling training. HR told her it was just part of the job.
stories.outcome: Settled — back injury, loss of earnings included

stories.name: James
stories.situation: Factory worker whose hand was caught in machinery that lacked a proper safety guard. The guard had been removed three weeks earlier for maintenance.

stories.name: Yvonne
stories.situation: Slipped on a wet floor in a hotel kitchen where no non-slip footwear had been provided despite repeated requests. Fractured her ankle.

subcategories.label: Medical Negligence or Hospital Error
subcategories.law: Medical practitioners owe a duty of care to patients. A claim arises when treatment falls below the standard expected of a reasonably competent practitioner and causes harm.
subcategories.time_limit: 2 years from the date you became aware of the negligence
subcategories.compensation_range: €30,000 to €500,000+ depending on the severity of harm caused.

stories.name: Mary
stories.situation: Diagnosed with cancer six months later than she should have been due to a missed reading on a scan. Her prognosis changed significantly as a result.
stories.outcome: Case settled — hospital admitted delayed diagnosis

stories.name: Paul
stories.situation: Underwent a routine operation after which he developed a serious infection. It later emerged standard post-operative protocols had not been followed.

stories.name: Fatima
stories.situation: Given incorrect medication by a hospital pharmacy. The error was not caught until she had an adverse reaction requiring further treatment.

stories.name: Declan
stories.situation: A surgical procedure left him with nerve damage that was later found to be a result of poor technique rather than an unavoidable complication.

subcategories.label: Injured on Someone Else's Property
subcategories.law: Occupiers Liability Act 1995 — property owners must take reasonable care to ensure their premises are safe for visitors.
subcategories.time_limit: 2 years from the date of the incident
subcategories.compensation_range: €10,000 to €75,000 depending on injury and circumstances.

stories.name: Louise
stories.situation: Fell through a rotting wooden step at a rental property she was viewing. The landlord was present and had not flagged any hazard.
stories.outcome: Claim successful — landlord's insurance paid out

stories.name: Conor
stories.situation: Injured when a garden gate collapsed on him while visiting a friend. The latch had been broken for months.

stories.name: Elena
stories.situation: Tripped on uneven paving at a private car park. The hazard was not marked and had been reported to the owner previously.

stories.name: Tomasz
stories.situation: Was hit by a falling object at a neighbour's property during renovation work. No safety barriers had been erected.

subcategories.label: Cycling Accident or Pedestrian Knocked Down
subcategories.law: Road Traffic Act — motorists owe a duty of care to cyclists and pedestrians. Local authorities may also be liable for poor road or path maintenance.
subcategories.time_limit: 2 years from the date of the accident

stories.name: Roisin
stories.situation: Knocked off her bike by a car door being opened without warning. She fractured her collarbone and was off work for two months.

stories.name: Michal
stories.situation: Hit by a van while cycling in a designated cycle lane. The driver claimed he didn't see him.

stories.name: Grace
stories.situation: Knocked down on a pedestrian crossing by a driver who ran a red light. Suffered a broken leg and significant psychological trauma.

stories.name: Ibrahim
stories.situation: Cyclist who fell due to a large pothole that the council had been notified about six months earlier. No repair had been made.

subcategories.label: Victim of an Assault
subcategories.law: Victims of assault in Ireland can bring a civil claim for compensation in addition to any criminal proceedings. Claims can also be made to the Criminal Injuries Compensation Tribunal.
subcategories.time_limit: 2 years from the date of the assault
subcategories.compensation_range: €10,000 to €100,000+ depending on physical and psychological injuries.

stories.name: Derek
stories.situation: Attacked outside a pub by a stranger. Suffered a broken nose and significant anxiety following the incident. The attacker was identified and charged.
stories.outcome: Civil claim settled — Criminal Injuries Tribunal also awarded compensation

stories.name: Alina
stories.situation: Assaulted by a fellow employee at work. Her employer had previously received complaints about this person and taken no action.

stories.name: Shane
stories.situation: Attacked by a nightclub patron in circumstances where security staff failed to intervene despite the situation escalating visibly.
stories.outcome: Venue found liable — settled out of court

stories.name: Valentina
stories.situation: Victim of a random street assault that left her with lasting psychological effects. Did not know she could claim compensation through the Criminal Injuries Tribunal.
stories.outcome: Tribunal award made — solicitor guided full process

subcategories.label: Pothole or Road Defect Injury
subcategories.law: Local authorities have a duty under the Roads Act 1993 to maintain public roads. If they had notice of a defect and failed to repair it, they may be liable.
subcategories.time_limit: 2 years from the date of the injury — but notice to the council within 2 months is strongly advised

stories.name: Liam
stories.situation: Motorbike rider thrown from his bike after hitting a large pothole on a rural road. The pothole had been reported to the council four times over two years.

stories.name: Sandra
stories.situation: Cyclist fell and broke her wrist after her wheel caught in a cracked road surface outside a school.

stories.name: Bartosz
stories.situation: Pedestrian who twisted his ankle badly stepping into an unmarked sunken drain on a footpath. Required surgery.
stories.outcome: Settled — council accepted the path was substandard

stories.name: Nuala
stories.situation: Car tyre blew out hitting a large pothole at night on an unlit road. The impact caused her to lose control and hit a ditch. She suffered neck and back injuries.

subcategories.label: Sports Injury Due to Negligence
subcategories.law: Participants in sport accept ordinary risks of the game, but not risks created by negligence — unsafe facilities, inadequate supervision, or dangerous equipment.
subcategories.time_limit: 2 years from the date of the injury
subcategories.compensation_range: €10,000 to €80,000 depending on the nature and permanence of the injury.

stories.name: Cillian
stories.situation: Injured on a GAA pitch that had not been properly maintained. A concealed rut caused him to fall awkwardly, fracturing his ankle.

stories.name: Nikita
stories.situation: Gym member injured when a cable machine snapped due to poor maintenance. The equipment had not been serviced in over a year.

stories.name: Fiona
stories.situation: Participant in a organised 5k run who fell into an unmarked hole on the course route. The route had not been checked before the event.

stories.name: Dmitri
stories.situation: Swimming pool user who slipped on an untreated wet surface in the changing area and fractured his hip.

subcategories.label: Injury from a Defective Product
subcategories.law: Liability for Defective Products Act 1991 — manufacturers are liable for damage caused by defective products regardless of fault or negligence.
subcategories.time_limit: 3 years from the date of injury or the date you became aware the product caused the injury
subcategories.compensation_range: €10,000 to €200,000+ depending on the severity of injury caused.

stories.name: Anita
stories.situation: Suffered burns when a faulty electric blanket overheated. The product had already been subject to a recall she had not been notified about.

stories.name: Ronan
stories.situation: Food poisoning caused by contaminated packaged food bought from a supermarket. A batch issue was later confirmed by the FSAI.

stories.name: Svetlana
stories.situation: Injured when a children's toy broke apart and caused a laceration requiring stitches. The product failed EU safety standards.

stories.name: Cormac
stories.situation: Power tool malfunctioned during normal use causing serious hand injuries. A design fault was identified in the same model across multiple units.

subcategories.label: Holiday or Travel Accident
subcategories.law: Package Travel Regulations 2019 — tour operators are liable for the proper performance of all services in a package holiday. Claims for overseas accidents can often be brought in Ireland.
subcategories.time_limit: 2 years — though this varies depending on where the accident occurred
subcategories.compensation_range: €10,000 to €100,000+ depending on injury and circumstances.

stories.name: Rachel
stories.situation: Slipped by a hotel pool on a package holiday. The surface was wet and there was no warning sign. She fractured her wrist and her holiday was ruined.
stories.outcome: Tour operator liable — Irish court claim successful

stories.name: Tadgh
stories.situation: Food poisoning at an all-inclusive resort left him hospitalised for three days. Multiple guests from the same tour group were affected.

stories.name: Miriam
stories.situation: Injured on a excursion booked through a package holiday operator. The activity provider had no proper safety briefing or equipment checks.

stories.name: Andrei
stories.situation: Suffered a serious injury when a hired scooter had faulty brakes. He was not warned about the fault and had not been given a safety check before departure.
stories.outcome: Rental company and tour operator both found liable
